message:
test(fuzz): add CBOR text/byte-string canonical length-boundary fuzz target (resolves h4al7)

to_canonical_cbor MUST emit text strings (Major Type 3, head 0x60-0x7b)
and byte strings (Major Type 2, head 0x40-0x5b) in shortest-form per
RFC 8949 §4.2.1. Length-prefix table:
  len ∈ [0,23]            → 1+len bytes (embedded length)
  len ∈ [24,255]          → 2+len bytes (0x78/0x58 + 1-byte length)
  len ∈ [256,65535]       → 3+len bytes (0x79/0x59 + 2 bytes BE)
  len ∈ [65536,u32::MAX]  → 5+len bytes (0x7a/0x5a + 4 bytes BE)

Existing cbor_int_canonical_length (lwzfk) covers integer length
boundaries but NOT string length encoding. A regression to
longer-than-shortest length encodings would let attackers smuggle
non-canonical wire forms past the canonical encoding gate.

Four properties driven over a length-range discriminator:
- text length boundary
- byte-string length boundary
- head-byte major-type binding (text=3, bytes=2)
- ciborium round-trip preserves type tag and content

Once-gated regression anchors verify exact byte prefixes for boundary
lengths {0, 23, 24, 255, 256, 65535} for both text and byte strings:
- "" → 0x60; "a"*23 → 0x77; "a"*24 → 0x78 0x18; "a"*256 → 0x79 0x01 0x00
- [] → 0x40; [0]*23 → 0x57; [0]*24 → 0x58 0x18; [0]*256 → 0x59 0x01 0x00

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -759,5 +759,12 @@ test = false

+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_cbor_string_canonical_length"
+path = "fuzz_targets/cbor_string_canonical_length.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
